sch:pin RECORD=2 — Unknown1/Reserved byte at offset 4: read and discarded by both us and AltiumSharp; always 0x00 in all goldens; purpose undecoded.

sch:pin RECORD=2 — FormalType != 1: code-level round-trip bug is confirmed, but NO golden in the available TestData has FormalType != 1; the 'STM32 GPIO pins show FT=0' claim is not reproducible from available files (needs the external STM32 MCU library or live Altium to demonstrate FT=0).

sch:pin RECORD=2 — non-empty SwapIdGroup / SwapIdPart and the 'no swap field' (empty) case: every golden uses exactly '|&|' (empty part/seq, field present), so non-empty/absent encodings are confirmed only via AltiumSharp code, not real bytes.
sch:pin RECORD=2 — PinFrac and PinSymbolLineWidth aux-stream per-entry (zlib) layouts: decoded by AltiumSharp but not byte-verified here; no golden pin used a non-zero coordinate fraction.
sch:rectangle RECORD=14 — plain LINESTYLE (non-Ext) on a rectangle: 0/26 goldens carry it; whether live Altium ever emits a plain LINESTYLE on a rectangle is unsettled.

sch:rectangle RECORD=14 — LineWidth enum->mil endpoints (index 2/3 = Large/Largest) and the rectangle UI max: all golden rects use index 0/1; the index->mil table is AltiumSharp inference, not byte-derived.
sch:rectangle RECORD=14 — non-zero OWNERINDEX on a SchLib (vs SchDoc) rectangle: 0/26 goldens; unsettled.

sch:rectangle RECORD=14 — IsSolid=T + Transparent=T render semantics: the byte combination is confirmed legal (2 goldens) but how Altium renders a transparent-yet-solid fill is a display question, not an I/O one.
sch:rectangle RECORD=14 — *_FRAC on a rectangle: 0/26 goldens exercise it (the feature is real on other primitives); do not expect a golden rectangle _FRAC fixture.

sch:line RECORD=13 — LineStyleExt for sch:line: AltiumSharp writes it (write-only, not in the line DTO) when LineStyle!=0; no golden line has a non-solid LineStyle, so whether live Altium emits/consumes it for lines is unsettled.
sch:line RECORD=13 — LineWidth index 3 ('Largest', 6 mil): all golden lines use index 1; the index-3 width is not byte-confirmed.
sch:arc RECORD=12 — LineWidth_Frac on a circular arc: SchArcDto has none and no golden carries it; a hypothetical live-Altium LineWidth_Frac would be undecoded by both.
sch:arc RECORD=12 — sub-0.001-degree StartAngle/EndAngle rounding beyond F3: all corpus angles are exact multiples of 30 degrees.

sch:ellipse RECORD=8 — version gating of Transparent/OwnerPartDisplayMode and the high-byte/alpha semantics of the 32-bit Color: not exercised (all samples same generator version, high byte always 0).

sch:bezier RECORD=5 — whether real Altium ever writes a binary/extra common token (GraphicallyLocked/Disabled/Dimmed/AreaColor/OwnerPartDisplayMode) on a bezier: the two goldens exercise only defaults.

sch:bezier RECORD=5 — no on-disk golden with a zero-omitted bezier coordinate exists (the (0,0)-start case lives only in an in-memory test); the omit-when-zero read bug is proven from source, not bytes.

sch:polyline RECORD=6 — LineStyleExt extended (>2) dash palette; full StartLineShape/EndLineShape/LineShapeSize shape-id enumeration; whether live Altium populates fill (Transparent/AreaColor/IsSolid) on an open polyline; OwnerIndex on a nested/multi-part library polyline.

sch:polygon RECORD=7 — LineWidth=3 meaning: goldens store LineWidth=3 but AltiumSharp's LineWidths[] table has only indices 0-2 and clamps idx>=3 to Small (1 mil); the true index-3 width (likely 6 mil 'Largest') needs live Altium.
sch:polygon RECORD=7 — absolute SchLib vertex unit (1 on-disk unit = ? mil): round-trip is self-consistent but no golden ties a vertex to a known physical dimension.
sch:polygon RECORD=7 — whether RECORD=7 vertices can carry X{n}_FRAC/Y{n}_FRAC: DTO declares them but no golden polygon emits them.

sch:roundrect RECORD=10 — internal-units vs DXP consistency across schlib primitives (RoundRect uses DXP, some neighbours like elliptical-arc radius use raw/100000): a unit-consistency audit is warranted; not a live-Altium gap per se but flagged for verification.

sch:ellipticalarc RECORD=11 — LineWidth coordinate-vs-index: AltiumSharp treats it as a Coord for RECORD=11 (not an index); whether real Altium stores a coord or an index coincides for the values seen (1,2); the high-byte/alpha of COLOR/AREACOLOR is unexercised; whether RECORD=11 can carry IsSolid/Transparent (filled/pie-like arc) is unproven.

sch:designator-parameter RECORD=34/41 — READONLYSTATE values >1, PARAMTYPE enum effect, AUTOPOSITION 1-4 geometry, TEXTHORZANCHOR/TEXTVERTANCHOR enums, ISSYSTEMPARAMETER/ISCONFIGURABLE/ISRULE behavioural effect: stored as raw ints with no decoded semantics; whether RECORD=34 can ever omit Location.X/Y in some Altium versions.

sch:implementation RECORD=44/45/46/47/48 — every TestData SchLib (incl. real-Altium vendor files) has only an empty RECORD=44, so NO populated implementation exists to settle: (a) the MODELDATAFILEKIND/ENTITY 0-vs-1 index base in genuine Altium, (b) whether genuine Altium writes OwnerIndex on SchLib RECORD=45/46/48, (c) the real key name for the PcbLib path (our 'ModelDatafile0'), (d) the RECORD=48 ImplementationParameters payload when non-empty, (e) whether Altium omits or writes ISCURRENT=F for non-current models.

sch:component RECORD=1 — RecordFrame high-byte flag values beyond 0x00 (text) and 0x01 (pin); the FULL optional managed-component key set and canonical order (vault/revision GUIDs etc. are absent from the corpus and AltiumSharp's writer order disagrees with golden); whether a SchLib component root ever carries a non-default LOCATION.X/Y; COMPONENTKIND/LIBIDENTIFIERKIND/VARIANTOPTION enum value->meaning; AreaColor/Color vs OVERIDECOLORS precedence; and the exact per-library FileHeader Weight formula.
